description: GET /communications/rfqs accepts optional subaccount query parameter. CreateRfqRequest already has subaccount, but ListRfqsParams is missing it. Add subaccount field, builder method, and query string support.

description:
Compare Order and CreateOrderRequest fields against latest spec. Known areas to verify:

1. Order struct: verify all response fields are present, especially:
   - event_ticker (present in API responses per spec, may be missing in our struct)
   - Check if any new fields were added

2. CreateOrderRequest: The docs show 'action' (buy/sell) as separate from 'side' (yes/no). Verify our model correctly separates these.

3. DecreaseOrderRequest: verify reduce_by/reduce_to vs spec's simpler 'count' field

4. BatchCreateOrdersResponse / BatchCancelOrdersResponse: verify error handling schema matches

5. AmendOrderRequest: verify required/optional field alignment

Files: src/models/order.rs, src/models/common.rs
title: Audit Order/CreateOrderRequest models against API spec

description:
Compare Market struct fields against the latest docs.kalshi.com spec. Known items to verify:

Fields in spec that may be missing or different in our model:
- Verify all dollar-denominated fields have _dollars suffix versions
- Check if settlement_value_dollars and settlement_value (cents) are both present
- Verify price_ranges PriceRange struct matches spec (start/end/step in dollars)
- Check fractional_trading_enabled field presence
- Verify can_close_early, early_close_condition
- Check for any new fields added since last update

The Market model is large (~50+ fields). Do a systematic field-by-field comparison against the OpenAPI Market schema.

Files: src/models/market.rs (Market struct, ~line 154-343)
title: Audit Market model fields against latest API spec

description:
The API spec and Python SDK show settlement_status as a query parameter for GET /portfolio/positions. Enum values: all, unsettled, settled. Default: unsettled.

Our GetPositionsParams in src/models/position.rs is missing this field.

Also verify if the FCM positions endpoint shares this param (it does per the spec).
title: Add settlement_status param to GetPositionsParams

description:
Download the official OpenAPI spec from https://docs.kalshi.com/openapi.yaml and save it to the repo (e.g., specs/openapi.yaml) for ongoing comparison.

Consider writing a script or test that parses the OpenAPI YAML and validates our Rust types against it, to catch drift automatically.

This would make future audits much faster.
title: Download OpenAPI YAML spec for automated comparison

description:

Channels to verify:
- orderbook (snapshot + delta)
- ticker
- trade (public trades)
- user_orders
- user_fills
- market_lifecycle
- communications (RFQ/quote updates)
- market_positions
- order_group_updates
- multivariate_lookups

Check for:
- New fields on existing message types
- New channels not yet supported
- send_initial_snapshot option support
- market_id field presence on all relevant types

description: Bring WebSocket implementation into full compliance with the official Kalshi AsyncAPI v2.0.0 spec. Covers missing message types, field mismatches, protocol gaps, and channel configuration.
title: AsyncAPI spec compliance epic

description:
Several DELETE endpoints return parsed response bodies but the API spec says they return 204 No Content:

- delete_api_key() returns DeleteApiKeyResponse but spec says 204 No Content
- cancel_rfq() returns RfqResponse but spec says 204 No Content  
- cancel_quote() returns QuoteResponse but spec says 204 No Content
- accept_quote() returns QuoteResponse but spec says 204 No Content

These should use http.delete() or http.put_no_content() instead of delete_with_response/put.

Files: src/api/api_keys.rs, src/api/communications.rs, src/models/api_key.rs, src/models/communications.rs
title: Fix DELETE endpoint response types to match API spec

description: GET /portfolio/order_groups and GET /portfolio/order_groups/{id} accept optional subaccount query parameter. Add subaccount to GetOrderGroupsParams (with builder + query string), update get_order_group and list_order_groups API functions, and wire up in client.rs.
title: Add subaccount param to order group read endpoints

description:
Our HistoricalCutoffResponse uses String for timestamp fields (market_settled_ts, trades_created_ts, orders_updated_ts), but the OpenAPI spec defines them as int64.

Need to verify the actual API response format and decide:
- If API returns integers: change to i64
- If API returns ISO8601 strings: keep as String but note the spec discrepancy
- Consider using serde(untagged) or a flexible timestamp type

File: src/models/historical.rs
title: Verify HistoricalCutoffResponse field types (String vs i64)

description:
The Communications API has the most complex divergence from the simplified OpenAPI spec. Need to verify our rich model (which uses market_ticker, contracts, target_cost_centi_cents, rest_remainder etc.) matches the ACTUAL API behavior vs the OpenAPI summary which shows simpler fields (ticker, side, count, counterparty_user_ids).

Key investigation needed:
- Verify CreateRfqRequest field names match actual API (market_ticker vs ticker)
- Verify CreateQuoteRequest fields (our yes_bid/no_bid vs spec's simpler price/count)
- Verify AcceptQuoteRequest fields (our accepted_side + contracts vs spec's just count)
- Check if counterparty_user_ids is a missing field on CreateRfqRequest

Files: src/models/communications.rs, src/api/communications.rs

Cross-reference: https://docs.kalshi.com/api-reference/communications/
title: Audit Communications API (RFQ/Quotes) models against latest spec

description:
The OpenAPI spec references mve_filter for GET /historical/markets. One version of the docs mentions mve_historical_filter. Need to verify the exact parameter name accepted by the API.

Our code uses mve_filter in GetHistoricalMarketsParams. This may be correct but should be verified against the actual API behavior.

File: src/models/historical.rs

description:
The API spec shows GetBalanceResponse has a nullable locked_balance field (integer, in cents) that we don't model. This field represents funds locked in pending orders.
